capture_blocks.objective: verify that the path remains stable before the repeatability baseline starts
operator_instructions: Confirm that the full reset has already been completed before starting.
Stay outside the controlled path between AP and ESP32-C5.
If the path looks less stable than in the earlier precursor, stop and record the issue explicitly.
capture_blocks.scenario: post-reset technical warm-up and transport sanity check

capture_blocks.objective: capture the repeated empty reference under the same L1 stand geometry
operator_instructions: Leave the controlled 2.00 m path between AP and ESP32-C5.
If possible, leave the room or stand far enough to avoid affecting the radio path.
Keep the room layout unchanged.

capture_blocks.objective: reproduce the static-presence reference from the earlier precursor
operator_instructions: Enter the midpoint of the controlled path between AP and ESP32-C5.
Reproduce the same standing position used in the earlier precursor as closely as practical.
Do not touch the chair, AP, table, or cables.
capture_blocks.scenario: repeated static presence in the controlled path

capture_blocks.objective: reproduce the slow-motion reference from the earlier precursor
operator_instructions: Stay within the same controlled path between AP and ESP32-C5.
Repeat the same slow-motion pattern used in the earlier precursor as closely as practical.
Do not change the topology of the stand.
capture_blocks.scenario: repeated slow motion in the controlled path

capture_blocks.objective: check the reverse transition back to empty after the repeated cycle
operator_instructions: Leave the controlled path again.
If possible, leave the room or stand far enough to avoid affecting the radio path.
Keep the room layout unchanged.
capture_blocks.scenario: empty scene recovery after the first repeated motion cycle

capture_blocks.objective: give a second static-presence evidence point inside the repeat session
operator_instructions: Return to the midpoint of the controlled path.
Stand almost still in approximately the same position as in S1.
Avoid any extra motion not needed for the block.
capture_blocks.scenario: second repeated static presence in the same controlled path

capture_blocks.objective: give a second slow-motion evidence point inside the repeat session
operator_instructions: Stay within the same controlled path used in M1.
Repeat the same slow motion pattern as closely as practical.
Do not move chairs, table, AP, ESP32-C5, or cables.
capture_blocks.scenario: second repeated slow motion in the same controlled path

capture_blocks.objective: finish the repeat session with a final empty reference
operator_instructions: Leave the controlled path one final time.
If possible, leave the room or stand far enough to avoid affecting the radio path.
Keep the room layout unchanged until the capture is stopped.
capture_blocks.scenario: final empty recovery after the repeatability run

config.description: D01 repeatability baseline on the current single-link stand under honest L1 conditions.
operator_notes: Repeatability baseline captured on the same physical stand as the earlier C1 precursor.
Apply a full reset before W0 and treat this as an independent repeat session.
Keep the primary prplOS-compatible dual-band AP node on 5 GHz / channel 36 / 20 MHz.
Do not move chairs, table, AP node, capture node, or cables during the capture sequence.

honesty_notes: Repeatability needs an independent valid run, not only reuse of the earlier E01 precursor.

scripts.description: Check local WSL/Linux dependencies and capture-device visibility.
Apply the requested router radio, SSID, and AccessPoint settings.
Collect router-side snapshots over SSH or serial console.
Build the configured ESP32 firmware workspace.
Flash the configured ESP32 firmware workspace.
Capture the ESP32 serial stream for the configured duration.
Run a continuous serial capture and pause for operator confirmation between configured capture blocks.
Run preflight, AP setup, AP snapshot, and then the interactive operator-guided capture sequence.
